skill.name: google-maps-api-skill
skill.description: Automate extraction of business data from Google Maps using the BrowserAct API. Retrieve business names, addresses, phone numbers, ratings, and website URLs for lead generation and market research.
skill.summary: Scrape Google Maps business data automatically for lead generation and market research.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 52 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The 22 external_commands findings in SKILL.md are markdown inline code formatting (single backticks around terms like `keywords`) misidentified as Ruby backtick execution. The 4 weak cryptographic algorithm findings point to plain markdown text with no cryptographic operations. Network and env_access findings reflect legitimate API calls to BrowserAct and standard environment variable usage for the BROWSERACT_API_KEY. The skill is a legitimate Google Maps data extraction tool with no evidence of malicious intent, obfuscation, or data exfiltration. It is rated LOW risk because it makes external API calls and accesses credential environment variables, which carry inherent but acceptable risk for a skill of this type.

content.user_title: Extract Google Maps Business Data for Lead Generation
content.value_statement: Manually collecting business data from Google Maps is slow and error-prone. This skill automates the extraction of business names, addresses, phone numbers, ratings, and website URLs from Google Maps using the BrowserAct API.
seo_keywords: Google Maps API skill Claude Code
actual_capabilities: Extract business names, categories, addresses, and phone numbers from Google Maps search results
Retrieve website URLs, star ratings, and review counts for any searchable business type
Filter Google Maps searches by custom keywords, UI language, and country location bias
Monitor business operational status across multiple regions and industries
Generate structured lead lists with contact information for sales outreach campaigns
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set as BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Task execution can take several minutes depending on search scope and API response time
Limited to one automatic retry on failure before reporting the error to the user
Cannot bypass Google Maps legal terms, service rate limits, or regional data restrictions

use_cases.title: Sales Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Generate targeted lists of potential clients in specific industries and locations. Extract phone numbers and websites for direct sales outreach.
use_cases.target_user: Sales professionals and business development teams

use_cases.title: Competitor Market Research
use_cases.description: Analyze competing businesses in any neighborhood by extracting their ratings, business categories, and contact details for strategic planning.

use_cases.title: Local Service Discovery
use_cases.description: Find specific types of service providers such as dental clinics or restaurants with complete contact information in any supported region.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Business Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find coffee shops in San Francisco using the Google Maps API skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user looking for a simple business type search in one city

prompt_templates.title: Language-Specific Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for hotels in Tokyo with language set to ja and country set to jp using the Google Maps API skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: User searching for businesses in a non-English market

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Lead Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all SaaS companies in Austin Texas using the Google Maps API skill. Get their phone numbers and website links.
prompt_templates.scenario: Sales user building a prospect list for a specific industry and location

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Region Market Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google Maps API skill to find dental clinics in three countries: us, gb, and ca. Compare average ratings across regions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user performing cross-region competitive analysis

output_examples.input: Extract all dental clinics in Austin Texas with contact details
output: 3 additional results found and formatted into a structured lead list

best_practices: Verify that the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before executing the script to avoid authentication errors
Use specific and targeted keywords for faster execution and more accurate results from the Google Maps search
Monitor terminal output during execution to track task progress and detect any authentication errors early
anti_patterns: Do not modify the script to bypass the single retry limit, as repeated failures indicate a persistent issue that needs human review
Do not attempt to access Google Maps directly instead of using the BrowserAct API, as this bypasses the intended workflow and may violate terms
Do not store or share scraped business data without verifying compliance with applicable data privacy and usage regulations

faq.question: What API key is needed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key set as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. Get your key from the BrowserAct Console.

faq.question: How long does a typical search take?
faq.answer: Execution usually takes several minutes. The script polls the API every 10 seconds and prints status logs with timestamps.

faq.question: What types of businesses can this skill search for?
faq.answer: Any business type that is searchable on Google Maps. Use keywords like restaurant, dental clinic, hotel, or SaaS company.

faq.question: What data fields are returned from a search?
faq.answer: The skill returns business name, primary category, full address, phone number, website URL, star rating, review count, and operational status.

faq.question: Can this skill bypass Google Maps rate limits or legal restrictions?
faq.answer: No. The skill works through the BrowserAct API and does not bypass Google Maps terms of service or rate limits.

faq.question: What happens if the script encounters an error?
faq.answer: The script performs one automatic retry. If the error persists, it stops and reports the specific error to the user for manual resolution.
